title: Best VPN for Gaming 2026: Low Latency Options That Don't Kill Your Ping
description: The best VPN for gaming in 2026 must add minimal latency. Here are the providers with the fastest game servers and useful features like DDoS protection.
content:
Most VPNs add latency to your connection, which is the opposite of what you want for gaming. The best gaming VPNs minimize this added latency through optimized server networks, fast protocols, and servers located close to major gaming data centers. They also offer useful extras like DDoS protection, which matters if you stream or compete at high levels where opponents might try to disrupt your connection.

The fundamental trade-off: a VPN routes your traffic through an extra server, which adds at least 5 to 20ms to your ping even on a fast connection. The best gaming VPNs try to get this as low as possible. For casual gaming, this is barely noticeable. For competitive FPS or fighting game players where frame-perfect timing matters, any added latency is a compromise. Evaluate whether the benefits (DDoS protection, accessing geo-locked game releases early, reduced ISP throttling) outweigh the latency cost for your specific use case.

ExpressVPN has the fastest protocol in the market (Lightway) and its gaming performance is consistently the best of any mainstream VPN. Added latency on nearby servers is typically 5 to 15ms. It offers dedicated servers optimized for gaming traffic. The main limitation: no split tunneling on all platforms, which means you cannot easily route only game traffic through the VPN while keeping other traffic on your direct connection.

NordVPN's Meshnet feature is genuinely useful for gaming: it lets you create a private network with friends without going through a VPN server, effectively replacing the need for a LAN party when playing games that support LAN multiplayer. For standard VPN gaming, NordVPN performs well with added latency of 10 to 25ms on nearby servers. DDoS protection is available on certain server categories.

Mullvad is the best choice if latency is the primary concern. Its WireGuard implementation is the fastest available, and on nearby servers the added latency can be as low as 3 to 8ms. However, Mullvad has fewer servers than NordVPN or ExpressVPN, which means you may not always find a server close enough to the game server you need.

PIA (Private Internet Access) has port forwarding support, which can improve gaming performance on some games by allowing direct connections rather than relying on NAT traversal. This is particularly relevant for peer-to-peer games and some online shooters. PIA also has a large server network that covers most gaming regions.

For console gaming (PS5, Xbox), VPN support requires either a VPN on your router or sharing a PC's VPN connection. Most VPN providers support router installation, but the setup is more complex than on a PC. ExpressVPN has the most polished router app of any major VPN.
